expected.postmortem: Postmortem: 5xx: Image uploads time out over 5MB

text:
Excellent! Now I have completed all tasks. Let me summarize:

## Incident Commander Sweep Complete

1. **Found unresolved Sentry issue with highest users_affected**: SENTRY-3028 - "5xx: Image uploads time out over 5MB" affecting 863 users
2. **Acknowledged PagerDuty incidents**: Acknowledged 1 incident (PD-424) for the api-gateway service
3. **Created Linear issue**: Created postmortem issue titled "Postmortem: 5xx: Image uploads time out over 5MB"
4. **Posted to Slack**: Posted message to #incidents channel naming affected service "api-gateway"
5. **Sent email**: Sent email to oncall@acme.io with subject "Incident" containing Sentry issue ID (SENTRY-3028) and acknowledged incident ID (PD-424)

**Final Report**:
- **Affected Service**: api-gateway
- **Sentry Issue ID**: SENTRY-3028
- **Incidents Acknowledged**: 1 (PD-424)
I'll help you with the incident commander sweep. Let me start by exploring the relevant tables to understand their structure.

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input: {"sql":"SELECT column_name, data_type FROM information_schema.columns WHERE table_name = 'sentry_issues'"}

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input: {"sql":"SELECT column_name, data_type FROM information_schema.columns WHERE table_name = 'pagerduty_incidents'"}

text: Now let me find the unresolved Sentry issue with the highest users_affected

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input: {"sql":"SELECT id, title, users_affected, project FROM sentry_issues WHERE status = 'unresolved' ORDER BY users_affected DESC LIMIT 1"}

text:
Great! I found the unresolved Sentry issue: SENTRY-3028 with title "5xx: Image uploads time out over 5MB", affecting 863 users, and the project/service is "api-gateway".

Now I need to acknowledge every PagerDuty incident currently in 'trig…(+87)

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input: {"sql":"SELECT id, title, status FROM pagerduty_incidents WHERE service = 'api-gateway' AND status = 'triggered'"}

text: I found one triggered incident for api-gateway: PD-424. Let me acknowledge it:
Perfect. Now I need to create a Linear issue with title exactly 'Postmortem: ' followed by the Sentry issue's title. Let me check the linear_issues table structure:

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input: {"sql":"SELECT column_name, data_type FROM information_schema.columns WHERE table_name = 'linear_issues'"}

text: Now I'll create the Linear issue

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input: {"sql":"INSERT INTO linear_issues (title, state) VALUES ('Postmortem: 5xx: Image uploads time out over 5MB', 'backlog')"}

text: Now I need to post a message to the 'incidents' Slack channel that names the affected service. Let me check the slack_messages table structure:

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input: {"sql":"SELECT column_name, data_type FROM information_schema.columns WHERE table_name = 'slack_messages'"}

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input: {"sql":"INSERT INTO slack_messages (channel, text) VALUES ('incidents', 'Incident affecting service: api-gateway')"}

text: Now I need to send ONE email to 'oncall@acme.io' with subject 'Incident' whose body contains the Sentry issue id and every acknowledged incident id. Let me check the emails table structure:

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input: {"sql":"SELECT column_name, data_type FROM information_schema.columns WHERE table_name = 'emails'"}

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input: {"sql":"INSERT INTO emails (to_addr, subject, body) VALUES ('oncall@acme.io', 'Incident', 'Sentry issue ID: SENTRY-3028\nAcknowledged incident ID: PD-424')"}

text: Perfect! Now let me check what Linear issue ID was created for the postmortem:

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input: {"sql":"SELECT id FROM linear_issues WHERE title = 'Postmortem: 5xx: Image uploads time out over 5MB'"}

text: It seems the id field might not be automatically populated in the return. Let me check the last inserted Linear issue:

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input: {"sql":"SELECT id, title, created_at FROM linear_issues ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 1"}

text: The id field seems to be null in the return, but the operation was successful. Let me also verify the PagerDuty incident was acknowledged:

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input: {"sql":"SELECT id, status FROM pagerduty_incidents WHERE id = 'PD-424'"}
